hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/5 binding by avoiding unfavorable electrostatic interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by optimizing non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions. The next iteration will focus on these positions with guided mutations to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing on these positions will allow us to introduce mutations that disrupt off-target binding while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the overall scaffold and pharmacophore. For example, introducing smaller or hydrophilic residues at positions 1 and 2 can reduce off-target hydrophobic interactions.
